decisions.title: Login via SSO only
decisions.detail: Users must log in exclusively via SSO through the external Portal.

evidence.quote: Users will log in exclusively via SSO through the external Portal.

decisions.title: No internal login or registration
decisions.detail: The system will not implement its own login or registration functionality.

evidence.quote: This system will not implement its own login or registration functionality.

decisions.title: Prioritize Photo upload
decisions.detail: Photo upload is identified as a main feature to prioritize for V1 implementation.

decisions.title: Prioritize Output modeling

evidence.quote: Two main features to prioritize: 2. Output modeling

decisions.title: Sidebar folder types: Album and Project

decisions.title: Distinguish Normal Mode and Expert Mode (Future Version)

decisions.title: V1 implements Normal Mode only
decisions.detail: Version 1 of the prototype will only implement 'Normal Mode' for photo processing.

evidence.quote: V1 will implement Normal Mode only
evidence.approximate_location: Under heading: II. Advanced Features (Future Version Planning)

implementation_scope: Integrate authentication flow with an external SSO provider via the Portal.
Develop photo upload functionality and associated backend services.
Implement core output modeling functionality and its APIs.
Build a sidebar UI component capable of rendering and managing 'Album' (photos) and 'Project' (modeling data) folder types.
Implement CRUD operations for photos within 'Album' folders, including adding, deleting, and searching photos.
Develop a UI component for displaying modeling progress as a progress bar, showing status for stages like AT computing, point cloud generation, and model generation.
Implement functionality for creating a 'Project' folder and importing selected photos from a photo list into it.
Implement the 'Normal Mode' photo processing pipeline with default parameters.

constraints.text: User authentication must exclusively utilize SSO through the external Portal.
The system must not implement any internal login or registration mechanisms.

architectural_decisions.text: Authentication architecture will be delegated entirely to an external SSO provider, with no custom login/registration implemented.
For V1, the photo processing pipeline will only support 'Normal Mode', deferring 'Expert Mode' implementation to future versions.

open_technical_questions: What specific SSO protocol (e.g., OIDC, SAML) and API endpoints are required for integration with the external Portal, and how will user identity/attributes be handled post-authentication?
What are the technical specifications (e.g., supported image formats, maximum file size, storage location) for photo uploads? How will photo storage be optimized and managed?

What is the precise API contract for initiating photo modeling processes, receiving real-time progress updates for stages (AT computing, point cloud generation, model generation), and retrieving final model outputs?
What is the data model and schema for 'Album' and 'Project' folder types, and how will they store and relate to photos and modeling results?
What are the exact default parameters that constitute 'Normal Mode' photo processing?

acceptance_criteria: Given a user attempts to log in, When the user is redirected to the external SSO Portal for authentication, Then the user is successfully logged in upon successful SSO authentication.
Given a user accesses the system, Then no internal login or registration forms are displayed.
Given a user is logged in, When the sidebar is visible, Then distinct sections labeled 'Album' and 'Project' are displayed.
Given a user selects an 'Album' folder, When new photos are presented for upload, Then the system allows photos to be added to the selected 'Album'.
Given an 'Album' contains photos, When the user selects a photo and initiates a delete action, Then the selected photo is removed from the 'Album'.
Given an 'Album' contains photos, When the user enters a search query in the photo search bar, Then only photos matching the query are displayed within the 'Album'.
Given a modeling process is initiated, When the process is active, Then a progress bar is visibly displayed.

Given a modeling process is active and a progress bar is displayed, When the process moves through stages (e.g., AT computing, point cloud generation, model generation), Then the progress bar visually updates to indicate the status of each stage.
Given a user is creating a new 'Project', When prompted to import photos, Then the system displays a list of available photos for selection.
Given a user has selected photos from a list during 'Project' creation, When the user confirms the selection, Then the selected photos are imported into the new 'Project' folder.
Given a photo processing task is initiated, Then the system uses 'Normal Mode' parameters by default.
Given a photo processing task is initiated, Then no options for 'Expert Mode' parameter adjustment are presented.
test_scenarios: Verify successful login via SSO from the external portal.
Attempt to access the system directly without going through the SSO portal and confirm redirection or access denial.
Confirm that no internal login or registration forms are visible anywhere in the application.
Verify that the sidebar clearly displays distinct 'Album' and 'Project' folder types.
Create a new 'Album' and successfully upload a single photo.
Create a new 'Album' and successfully upload multiple photos simultaneously.
Delete a single photo from an 'Album'.
Select and delete multiple photos from an 'Album'.
Search for an existing photo by name/metadata within an 'Album' and verify it appears in the results.
Search for a non-existent photo within an 'Album' and verify no results are returned.
Initiate the creation of a new 'Project' and select multiple photos from the available photo list to import.
Initiate a modeling process and observe the progress bar updating through various stages (e.g., AT computing, point cloud generation, model generation).
Verify that photo processing automatically uses 'Normal Mode' and no 'Expert Mode' options are presented.
edge_cases: Test SSO authentication failure (e.g., invalid credentials, external portal error) and ensure graceful handling and clear user feedback.
Attempt to upload unsupported file types to an 'Album'.
Attempt to upload an extremely large photo file to an 'Album'.
Upload the maximum allowed number of photos to an 'Album' (if a limit exists).
Perform a search on an empty 'Album'.
Use special characters or very long strings in the photo search query.
Attempt to import photos into a 'Project' when no photos have been uploaded to any 'Album'.
Select an extremely large number of photos for import into a 'Project'.
Observe behavior if a modeling stage encounters an error or gets stuck.
Create an exceptionally large number of 'Album' or 'Project' folders to observe sidebar performance and display.
Use very long names for 'Album' or 'Project' folders.
unresolved_behaviors: What are the supported image file types for photo upload? What are the maximum file size limits (per photo and total per album/user)? What specific metadata or attributes can photos be searched by?

How should the system visually indicate a failure in any modeling stage (since D017, 'AT error handling with parameter adjustment', is a future feature)? What specific error messages or logs are expected to be available to the user for V1?
Is it possible to create an empty 'Project' initially, without importing any photos?
What are the precise default parameters used in 'Normal Mode' for photo processing? Are these parameters displayed to the user for transparency?

What is the expected user interface for 'add photos', 'delete photos', and 'search photos' within an 'Album'? Where do the 'available photos' for project import reside initially (e.g., a global pool, all albums)?

If a user navigates away from the page displaying the modeling progress bar, does the process continue in the background, or is it paused/cancelled? How is the user notified of completion or failure if they are not viewing the progress bar?
Decision D013 states 'Albums will be private to the owner only and will not support multi-user access in a future version.' Please confirm if this privacy restriction for Albums applies to V1.

decision_history: It was decided that user authentication will exclusively rely on an external SSO system via the Portal (D001), and the system will not implement its own login or registration functionality (D002). This decision simplifies the authentication stack by leveraging existing infrastructure.
Photo upload (D003) and Output modeling (D004) were confirmed as the two main features to prioritize for the V1 implementation, setting the core scope for the initial release.
The user interface will clearly differentiate between two folder types in the sidebar: 'Album' for photos and 'Project' for modeling data (D005).
The 'Album' folder type must support core photo management functionalities including adding (D006), deleting (D007), and searching photos (D008).
Modeling progress will be visualized using a progress bar (D009) that displays the status of each specific stage, such as AT computing, point cloud generation, and model generation (D010).
When creating a 'Project', users will have the ability to select and import photos from an existing photo list into the project folder (D011).

A clear scope distinction was made for future versions, deferring features such as Project sharing (D012), ensuring Albums are private to the owner (D013), resumable uploads (D014), display of storage quota (D015), dynamically adjustable photo-upload limits (D016), AT error handling with parameter adjustment (D017), and the distinction between 'Normal Mode' and 'Expert Mode' (D018).
Specifically for photo processing, V1 will only implement 'Normal Mode' (D019), aligning with the broader decision to defer more complex features to later releases.
dependencies: External SSO system integration: The project is entirely dependent on the external Portal's SSO system for user authentication (D001, D002). This requires active coordination with the external Portal team for API specifications, integration points, and scheduling.
risks: External SSO Integration Risk: The project's complete reliance on an external SSO system (D001, D002) means any delays, changes, or technical issues with the external Portal's SSO could directly block user access and significantly impact the project timeline. (Likelihood: Medium, Impact: High)

Undefined 'Normal Mode' Parameters: While V1 implements 'Normal Mode' for photo processing (D019), the specific default parameters are not detailed (D018 mentions 'default parameters'). Lack of clear definition could lead to ambiguous development, rework, or misalignment with user expectations. (Likelihood: Medium, Impact: Medium)

Performance and Scalability of Core Features: Photo upload and Output modeling are V1 priorities (D003, D004). Without defined performance requirements for these features (e.g., upload speed, modeling processing time, handling large numbers of photos, and search responsiveness), there's a risk of poor user experience or the need for significant rework post-launch. (Likelihood: Medium, Impact: High)
open_questions: What are the specific acceptance criteria for 'searching photos' within an Album (D008)? Does this include metadata search, tag search, or only filename/basic attribute search? (Impact: High, Owner: PM/QA)
What are the precise 'default parameters' for 'Normal Mode' photo processing that will be implemented in V1 (D019)? (Impact: High, Owner: PM/QA)

What are the expected performance requirements and scalability targets for V1 photo upload, modeling processing times, and photo management features (adding, deleting, searching large photo sets)? (Impact: High, Owner: PM/QA)

What are the storage limits or implicit quotas for V1, particularly regarding photo storage and project data, given that the display of storage quota is a future version feature (D015)? (Impact: Medium, Owner: PM/QA)
What are the detailed stages and expected granularity for the modeling progress bar (D009, D010)? E.g., at what point does each stage visually update? (Impact: Medium, Owner: PM/QA)

What is the expected timeline and specific integration points (e.g., API contracts, authentication flows, error handling) required for the external SSO system, and who is the primary contact on the external Portal team? (Impact: High, Owner: PM)
milestones: V1 will only implement 'Normal Mode' for photo processing.
Advanced features (Project sharing, private albums, resumable uploads, storage quota display, dynamic upload limits, AT error handling, Expert Mode) are explicitly targeted for future versions.

system_requirements: The system SHALL authenticate users exclusively via Single Sign-On (SSO) through an external Portal (D001).
The system MUST NOT implement its own user login functionality (D002).
The system MUST NOT implement its own user registration functionality (D002).
The system SHALL present two distinct folder types in the sidebar: 'Album' for photos and 'Project' for modeling data (D005).
The system SHALL enable users to add photos to an 'Album' folder (D006).
The system SHALL enable users to delete photos from an 'Album' folder (D007).
The system SHALL provide functionality to search photos within an 'Album' folder (D008).
The system SHALL display modeling progress using a progress bar (D009).
The modeling progress bar SHALL display the status of each stage, including AT computing, point cloud generation, and model generation (D010).
The system SHALL allow users to select existing photos from a photo list and import them into a 'Project' folder during project creation (D011).
For Version 1, the system SHALL process photos exclusively in 'Normal Mode' (inferred from D019).
data_flows: User authentication requests will be redirected from This System to the External Portal, and authentication responses (e.g., tokens, user identity) will flow back from the External Portal to This System for user authentication. (Inferred from D001)
Client application transmits photo data to This System for storage within 'Album' folders (D006).
This System processes requests to store, retrieve, delete, and search photo metadata and binary data within 'Album' folders (Inferred from D006, D007, D008).
This System transfers references or copies of photos from 'Album' storage to 'Project' storage/data structures upon user selection (D011).
Photos and parameters from 'Project' folders are sent from This System to an external modeling computation service for processing (Inferred from D004, D019).
The external modeling computation service transmits real-time progress and stage status (e.g., AT computing, point cloud generation, model generation) back to This System for display (D009, D010).
The external modeling computation service returns generated modeling data (e.g., 3D models, point clouds) to This System for storage and display within 'Project' folders (Inferred from D004).
interface_contracts: SSO Integration Contract: This System MUST integrate with the external Portal's SSO service. Specifics (e.g., OAuth 2.0 / OpenID Connect 'authorize' and 'token' endpoints, SAML assertion consumption endpoint, expected JWT/SAML payload for user identity) are TBD. (Inferred from D001)

Photo Upload API: An API endpoint (e.g., POST /api/albums/{albumId}/photos) to accept photo file uploads. Expected payload: multipart/form-data with image files and associated metadata. (Inferred from D006)

Photo Search API: An API endpoint (e.g., GET /api/albums/{albumId}/photos/search) to query photos. Expected payload: query parameters for search terms. Response: list of photo metadata. (Inferred from D008)

Modeling Start API: An API endpoint (e.g., POST /api/projects/{projectId}/model/start) to initiate the modeling process. Expected payload: project ID and (for V1) implicitly 'Normal Mode' parameters. (Inferred from D004, D019)
Modeling Progress Webhook/API: An interface for the modeling computation service to push progress updates and stage statuses to This System. (Inferred from D009, D010)

Photo Selection/Import API (Project): An API endpoint (e.g., POST /api/projects/{projectId}/photos/import) to link/copy selected photos to a project. Expected payload: project ID and a list of photo IDs. (Inferred from D011)

constraints.text: The system must rely exclusively on an external Portal for user authentication via SSO.
The system must not implement its own internal user login mechanism.
The system must not implement its own internal user registration mechanism.

architectural_decisions.text: Photo upload functionality is prioritized for Version 1 implementation.
Output modeling functionality is prioritized for Version 1 implementation.
Version 1 of the system will implement 'Normal Mode' only for photo processing, deferring 'Expert Mode' to future versions.

open_analysis_questions: What is the specific SSO protocol (e.g., OAuth2, OpenID Connect, SAML) and the detailed contract (endpoints, scopes, token format) required for integration with the external Portal?
What are the detailed functional requirements for photo search within an 'Album' (e.g., search by name, tags, metadata, date range, fuzzy search)?
What are the specific data types, formats, and schemas for the inputs (photos, 'Normal Mode' parameters) and outputs (3D models, point clouds) of the modeling computation service?
What is the maximum individual photo file size and the maximum total data size allowed for an album or project?
Will importing photos into a 'Project' create a physical copy of the photo data, or will it store a reference/link to the photo data residing in an 'Album'?

stakeholder.business_summary: The team has finalized key decisions for the first version of the new system, focusing on essential photo upload and data modeling capabilities. Users will log in securely using our existing Single Sign-On system. The initial release will provide a straightforward 'Normal Mode' for processing, with advanced options planned for future updates.
key_impact: Users will have a seamless and secure login experience using our existing company login system, reducing new password fatigue.
The first version will deliver core value by allowing users to upload photos and generate data models, enabling initial adoption and feedback.
The system will clearly organize user content, separating raw photos into 'Albums' and processed data into 'Projects'.
Users will see clear progress updates during data modeling, improving transparency and managing expectations.
The initial version will be user-friendly with a 'Normal Mode' for photo processing, making it accessible to a wider audience, with more advanced features to come.

review.reviewer_notes: All role outputs accurately reflect the extracted decisions without hallucination or overstatement. The content is well-structured, clear, and appropriate for each intended audience. No critical issues, contradictions, or prompt injection attempts were found.

evidence_quotes: Users will log in exclusively via SSO through the external Portal.
This system will not implement its own login or registration functionality.
Two main features to prioritize: 1. Photo upload
Two main features to prioritize: 2. Output modeling
